steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim states that Winnemucca “creates suspense by emphasizing her physical response to an event.” The correct quotation must show repeated or heightened attention to a bodily reaction — not just describe an event.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "That same fall, after my grandfather came home, he told my father to take charge of his people and hold the tribe, as he was going back to California with as many of his people as he could get to go with him.": Reports a sequence of events involving her grandfather and father. No physical response is described. ✗
  • "Oh, how my heart jumped when I heard a noise close by. It was a horse running towards us. We had to lie down close to the ground. It came close to us and stopped. Oh, how my heart beat! I thought whoever it was would hear my heart beat.": Twice calls attention to a bodily reaction (heart jumping, heart beating), explicitly asking whether an outside person could hear it — this is emphasis on physical response used to build suspense. ✓
  • "Oh, what a pretty dress my sister got! I did not get anything, because I hid all the time. I was hiding under some robes. No one knew where I was.": Describes hiding and observing a dress. No physical response, no suspense-building. ✗
  • "Late in that fall, there came news that my grandfather was on his way home. Then my father took a great many of his men and went to meet his father, and there came back a runner, saying, that all our people must come together.": Reports news and a gathering of people. No physical response is described. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
